Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The prompt describes a process, but does not provide explicit constraints. Wait, the prompt says: Consider a complex order fulfillment process... Your task: Construct a Python dictionary representing the Log Skeleton for the given scenario. It doesnt give explicit constraints in bullet points. I need to infer constraints from the description?

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets analyze the description: When a new customer order arrives... it must be processed through a series of activities... Activities: RO, CA, RI, PI, QC, PO, GSL, D, IC, RP. The description only lists the activities and their basic meaning, does not specify any constraints among them. ... This implies a sequence, but not necessarily strict declarative constraints.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: In a typical order fulfillment process, activities are mostly sequential. We can define: Always Before: CA before RI, RI before PI, PI before QC, QC before PO, PO before GSL, GSL before D. Also maybe D before IC or IC before RP?

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: always_before: (CA, RO) (RI, CA) (PI, RI) (QC, PI) (PO, QC) (GSL, PO) (D, GSL) (IC, D) (RP, IC) directly_follows: (RO, CA) (CA, RI) (RI, PI) (PI, QC) (QC, PO) (PO, GSL) (GSL, D) (D, IC) (IC, RP) activ_freq: all {1}

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Lets double-check: The prompt says when a new customer order arrives... it must be processed through a series of activities to ensure the order is correctly assembled, packaged, and shipped. That implies all listed activities are part of the process.

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Given the lack of specific constraints, the most generic answer might be to set all sets empty except activ_freq... Alternatively, maybe the user forgot to include a list of constraints.
